content:
Jian Zhou (周健) You can call me Jack.
I am an independent researcher based in Shanghai, China. My research lies at the intersection of number theory, mathematical physics, and condensed matter theory.
Professional Life By day, I work as Director of Product and Game Producer at XD.INC (HKEX: 02400), a Hong Kong-listed gaming company. I lead cross-platform game development teams working with Unreal Engine 5.
Berlin Years (2017–2020) Research Motivation The fine-structure constant α has long fascinated me — this mysterious dimensionless number that Feynman himself called “one of the greatest damn mysteries of physics.” I have been driven by a simple question: can this constant admit an elegant and exact mathematical expression?
This pursuit has led me from α to the Riemann Hypothesis, from superconductivity to membrane models of particles, all connected through the deep arithmetic structure of the Bost-Connes system.
Contact Email: jackzhou.sci@gmail.com ORCID: 0009-0000-3536-9500 GitHub: JackZH26 Figshare: Jian Zhou X (Twitter): @jackzhj

title: Publications

content:
Research Directions 1. The Fine-Structure Constant α Central Question: Can the fine-structure constant α ≈ 1/137.036 admit an elegant and exact mathematical expression?
Key Result: A four-layer arithmetic decomposition predicts α⁻¹ = 137.035999046, agreeing with CODATA 2022 to 0.009 ppm — 9 parts per billion.
The four-layer structure:
Layer 1 — Geometric: 360/φ² (golden angle, circle optimization) Layer 2 — Arithmetic: −ln φ (Dirichlet regulator of ℚ(√5)) Layer 3 — Thermodynamic: τ = 4k_B T_CMB / E_R (CMB thermal energy) Layer 4 — Higher-order: Bernoulli generating functions / Seeley–DeWitt heat kernel corrections φ is uniquely selected by the Bose–Einstein self-reference equation n_BE(ln x) = x, providing a first-principles derivation from quantum statistical mechanics.
📄 Preprints: Four-Layer Structure | Three-Layer Version | α = φ²/360 Conjecture
⚙️ Status: Under review at International Journal of Theoretical Physics (IJTP)
2. The Riemann Hypothesis Approach: Bost-Connes system over ℚ(√5) with golden unitarity.
Key Results:
Strong log-concavity bound: (log Φ)″(u) ≤ −67.65 for all u ≥ 0 — new quantitative bound not previously in the literature Quantitative Turán inequalities derived from this bound Weil positivity evidence across 168 primes (tail bounds < 10⁻¹¹⁰) Uniform Jensen hyperbolicity and Gaussian growth control Research program with explicit gap identification 📄 Preprints: Strong Log-Concavity | Seven Perspectives on RH
⚙️ Status: Under review at Potential Analysis (Q1) and Experimental Mathematics. First arXiv paper published: arXiv:2603.27613
3. Spherical Membrane Model of Particles Idea: All quantum fields are vibration modes of S², distinguished by fiber bundle type.
Electrons (Paper I): Bound-state electrons as standing-wave patterns on concentric spherical membranes, with quantum numbers (n, ℓ, m) as vibrational modes. Free electrons emerge as single membranes with continuous spectra.
Photons (Paper II): Photons as expanding spherical membranes S²(t) with R(t) = ct, whose vibration modes are the vector spherical harmonics. Eight features of classical and quantum optics emerge naturally as geometric properties.
Unified fiber bundle classification:
Spin 0 → Scalar membrane (Higgs) Spin 1/2 → Spinor membrane (electrons) Spin 1 → Vector membrane (photons) Spin 2 → Tensor membrane (gravitons) ⚙️ Status: Electron membrane paper submitted to Foundations of Physics
4. Room-Temperature Superconductivity Approach: Two-channel Allen-Dynes framework combining phonon and spin-fluctuation coupling with quantum-metric superfluid stiffness.
Key Results:
Blind predictions for 19 materials achieve R²log = 0.96 across five orders of magnitude in Tc No-go theorem: quantum metric cannot directly modify electron-phonon coupling in single-band, isotropic superconductors 7 candidate materials with predicted Tc > 200 K identified 📄 Preprint: Two-Channel Allen-Dynes Framework
⚙️ Status: Under review at Superconductor Science and Technology
5. Number Theory & Mathematical Physics Additional contributions in number theory and mathematical physics:
Mahler Measures: New conjectures on special values of L-functions (DOI) Stokes Multipliers: Exact closed-form results via PSLQ algorithm (arXiv:2603.27613) Lattice Green Functions: Domb number decomposition in d ≥ 4 (DOI) 📊 Overall: 20 preprints on Figshare, 1 on arXiv, 400+ views, 330+ downloads. Papers under review at IJTP, Foundations of Physics, Potential Analysis, and Experimental Mathematics.
summary:
Research Directions

1. The Fine-Structure Constant α

Central Question: Can the fine-structure constant α ≈ 1/137.036 admit an elegant and exact mathematical expression?

Key Result: A four-layer arithmetic decomposition predicts α⁻¹ = 137.035999046, agreeing with CODATA 2022 to 0.009 ppm — 9 parts per billion.

The four-layer structure:

  • Layer 1 — Geometric: 360/φ² (golden angle, circle optimization)
  • Layer 2 — Arithmetic: −ln φ (Dirichlet regulator of ℚ(√5))
  • Layer 3 — Thermodynamic: τ = 4k_B T_CMB / E_R (CMB thermal energy)
  • Layer 4 — Higher-order: Bernoulli generating functions / Seeley–DeWitt heat kernel corrections

φ is uniquely selected by the Bose–Einstein self-reference equation n_BE(ln x) = x, providing a first-principles derivation from quantum statistical mechanics.
title: Research
